meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)
meta.title: AWS-MLE-1: SageMaker Preparation, MLOps and Monitoring

domains.name: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows

questions.stem: A team needs to **run managed preprocessing and evaluation code before training**. Which service or feature is the best fit?

options.explanation: It is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets
It is the managed capability for preprocessing, feature engineering, and evaluation workloads in SageMaker
It is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search
It is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

questions.stem:
Which service or feature is most directly associated with the following need?

- Goal: run managed preprocessing and evaluation code before training
- Constraint: use the managed vendor capability when possible

What should the team choose?

options.explanation: It is the managed capability for preprocessing, feature engineering, and evaluation workloads in SageMaker
It is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets
It is not the AWS feature intended for automated hyperparameter search
It is not the AWS feature intended for post-deployment drift and quality monitoring

It is not the best fit for offline batch scoring of large datasets
It is not the main SageMaker capability for bias and explainability analysis
It serves a different ML lifecycle need than managed preprocessing jobs

questions.stem: A team must review a credit-risk model for **pre-training bias across demographic groups** before deployment. Which SageMaker capability is the best fit?

options.explanation: SageMaker Clarify provides bias detection workflows that can be run before deployment and supports explainability analysis for model predictions.
Model Monitor focuses on post-deployment monitoring such as data quality, model quality, bias drift, and feature attribution drift on endpoints.
Batch Transform runs offline inference jobs; it does not calculate fairness metrics.
AWS Glue Data Quality validates datasets and rules, not model bias or feature-attribution explanations.

questions.stem: A model is already serving traffic from a SageMaker endpoint. The team wants **ongoing alerts when feature attributions or bias metrics drift over time**. Which capability should they implement?

options.explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor supports monitoring on deployed endpoints, including data-quality checks and specialized jobs for bias drift and feature attribution drift.

options.text: SageMaker Clarify processing job only before deployment
options.explanation: A one-time Clarify job before deployment does not provide continuous post-deployment drift monitoring.
Experiments tracks runs, parameters, metrics, and artifacts rather than live endpoint drift.
Automatic model tuning searches hyperparameters during training, not production monitoring.

questions.reference: [Monitor models for bias drift and feature attribution drift](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/clarify-model-monitor-bias-drift.html)

questions.stem: An ML engineer must explain **which input features most influenced an individual prediction** for an auditor. Which SageMaker capability should the engineer use first?

options.explanation: SageMaker Clarify can produce feature-attribution explanations, such as SHAP-based explanations, to show which features most influenced a prediction.
Pipelines orchestrates ML workflow steps but does not itself generate prediction explanations.
Batch Transform performs offline inference without built-in explainability outputs.
A crawler infers dataset schema and catalog metadata, not model explanations.

questions.stem: A regulated ML workload needs **both pre-deployment bias analysis and feature-attribution explainability** using a managed SageMaker capability. Which option best fits?

options.explanation: SageMaker Clarify is the managed SageMaker capability for both bias analysis and model explainability workflows.
Model Registry governs package versions and approval states, not fairness or feature-attribution calculations.
Generic processing jobs can run arbitrary code but do not provide the managed Clarify-specific bias and explainability workflow asked for here.
Batch Transform is for batch inference rather than fairness analysis or explanation generation.
